ops.title: User wants remaining daemonkit/migration work parallelized as hard as possible; sequential lanes felt too slow
ops.body:
On the daemonkit extraction (2026-07-18), the user said execution was "going a bit too slow" and to "parallelize as much work as possible in the follow-up plan."

**Why:** the session's real bottlenecks were (1) implementation lanes serialized on the single shared daemonkit working copy, and (2) background `Agent` lanes that finished their codex work but went idle without relaying via `SendMessage`, so the orchestrator waited on nothing.

**How to apply:**
- Author every follow-up plan phase as a `Workflow` fan-out with the widest parallel wave the dependency graph allows — not sequential single-`Agent` dispatches. Independent repos and independent files are parallel by default.
- Give edit lanes `isolation: 'worktree'` so multiple implementers touch the daemonkit tree at once without colliding; recombine + verify after.
- Prefer the `Workflow` channel over background `Agent`s for delegated work whose result you need: workflow returns are deterministic (journal + return value), background-agent `SendMessage` relay silently failed here. If using background agents, require the result via `SendMessage` and nudge once before redoing; recover from disk, since the working copy/reply files are the real record.
- Migration tracks with no cross-dependency (e.g. Track A authkit, daemonkit v0.2 drain hardening, P4 Swift package, P2b supervise) run in one parallel wave, not one after another.

Related: [[daemonkit-extraction]]
